text.title: figma-ai-ecosystem Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for figma-ai-ecosystem as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Fetching the full file JSON for complex Figma files.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Figma file JSON can be 50–500 MB for complex files. Always request specific node subtrees using the ?ids=node_id parameter. Fetching the full file at scale will exhaust rate limits and memory.

failure-mode.description: Using Figma Sites published URLs as canonical references without re-checking after file changes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Figma Sites published URLs do not reflect file changes until a human manually re-publishes. Agents monitoring the URL may audit stale content if they assume the URL is always current.

failure-mode.description: Diffing a Figma file from a webhook event without scoping to changed node subtrees.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Figma Webhooks send file-level events, not node-level. An agent cannot know which specific frame changed without diffing the full file before and after — which is expensive for large files. Scope webhook reactions to specific frame ranges.

failure-mode.description: Working on original assets with Figma AI Image Tools instead of copies.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Figma AI image edits (erase, isolate, expand) are not reversible after file save. Always work on copies of assets. Confirm with the designer before applying destructive image operations.
